title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.
thesisTitle: The AI Thesis: The Everything Engine Runs on Cash
thesisParagraphs: Amazon (AMZN) is a beast of two worlds: global retail logistics and enterprise cloud infrastructure (AWS). After years of massive capital expenditures to double its fulfillment network and build out data centers, the company is shifting from an investment phase to a harvesting phase.

While the retail business operates on razor-thin margins, its sheer volume provides the float. AWS, on the other hand, is a high-margin money printer. With the explosion of generative AI workloads, AWS is positioned to dominate the foundational compute layer. The combination of regionalizing its U.S. fulfillment network (slashing costs to serve) and accelerating AWS revenue translates to an explosive inflection point in Free Cash Flow (FCF). The market hasn't fully digested the scale of this cash generation machine.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: 18% is a bold projection for a company of this size, but Amazon's FCF is deeply leveraged to its highest-margin businesses: AWS and Advertising. As AI workloads scale exponentially, AWS margins will expand. Simultaneously, cost efficiencies in logistics are dropping straight to the bottom line. This represents a structural shift in their cash conversion cycle.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.8% discount rate reflects Amazon's unique position. While it operates in competitive markets, its infrastructure (both digital and physical) constitutes an almost insurmountable moat. Factoring in a 4.18% risk-free rate, a beta of ~1.15, and the company's massive scale, the risk of permanent capital impairment is remarkably low.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 4.0% terminal growth rate assumes Amazon grows slightly faster than global GDP in perpetuity. Because AWS and Amazon Logistics are increasingly becoming the default operating systems for the modern digital and physical economies, they command long-term pricing power and structural growth advantages.

dcfIntroText: Starting with the FY2025 base FCF of {baseFCF}, the table below outlines the 5-year projection discounted back to Present Value (PV) using the {discountRate} WACC.
terminalIntroText: The Terminal Value captures the immense ongoing cash generation of Amazon from Year 6 into perpetuity, calculated using the Gordon Growth Model.

sensitivityIntroText: Given the scale of Amazon's cash flows, small changes in the WACC or Terminal Growth Rate have massive implications. The matrix below highlights valuation variance across different assumptions.

risks.introText: Even a dominant player like Amazon is exposed to systemic and operational risks that could derail this valuation:

items.title: 1. AWS Growth Deceleration
items.description: If Microsoft Azure or Google Cloud aggressively undercut AWS on pricing, or if the anticipated AI workload boom fails to materialize at the projected scale, Amazon's highest-margin engine will sputter, radically altering the FCF trajectory.

items.title: 2. Regulatory and Antitrust Action
items.description: Amazon faces persistent scrutiny regarding monopolistic practices in its e-commerce marketplace. Significant regulatory intervention could force structural changes or breakups, damaging its integrated business model.

items.title: 3. Consumer Spending Contraction
items.description: A prolonged macroeconomic downturn or recession would directly impact retail sales. While AWS provides a buffer, Amazon's vast fulfillment infrastructure still requires massive volume to maintain profitability.

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick an 18% growth rate for Amazon?
faq.answer: Gemini projects an 18% Free Cash Flow growth rate because Amazon's high-margin AWS business is scaling massively with AI workloads, and its massive investments in regionalizing its fulfillment network are significantly reducing cost-to-serve, unlocking unprecedented cash generation.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for Amazon's DCF?
faq.answer: An 8.8% discount rate was selected. This reflects Amazon's entrenched economic moat, diversified revenue streams across commerce and cloud, and a 4.18% risk-free rate.

faq.question: Why is Amazon's terminal growth rate set at 4.0%?
faq.answer: A 4.0% terminal growth rate reflects that Amazon's cloud and logistics infrastructure are fundamentally woven into the global economy, allowing it to sustain growth slightly above standard GDP rates in perpetuity.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).
